data.title: Capital A, a company founded by AirAsia's founder, is partnering with Standard Chartered Bank to explore issuing a stablecoin backed by the Malaysian Ringgit.
data.description: AirAsia's operator has signed an agreement with Standard Chartered Bank Malaysia to explore issuing a ringgit-backed stablecoin in the Southeast Asian country. This comes days after a member of the Malaysian royal family announced a similar token. According to a statement released Friday, Capital A, founded by AirAsia founder Tony Fernandes, signed a letter of intent with Standard Chartered Bank Malaysia to jointly develop and test the stablecoin through the Digital Asset Innovation Centre regulated by Bank Negara Malaysia. The statement indicates that Standard Chartered Bank Malaysia will act as the issuer of the stablecoin, while Capital A may initially pilot wholesale use cases in real-world scenarios. This marks Capital A's first foray into the regulated digital asset space. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Global Shift Towards Innovation in Crypto Regulation Predicted by 2025
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Elliptic's latest report, '2025 Global Crypto Regulatory Review,' highlights a significant shift in global crypto regulation from enforcement to innovation this year. The report suggests that banks, stablecoins, and Asian financial centers will lead the next phase of policy development.

The United States is experiencing the most notable changes, with U.S. President Donald Trump prioritizing crypto as a core policy issue. This includes the promotion of the GENIUS Act, a federal stablecoin framework, and the Department of Justice halting its approach of prosecuting instead of regulating. Additionally, the Securities and Exchange Commission has established a special task force to boost confidence in American crypto innovation.

The report indicates that stablecoins are evolving from centralized IOUs to native on-chain assets, serving as collateral, settlement, and yield tools, which impacts protocol design. Banks in the United States, European Union, and Hong Kong are preparing to enter stablecoin issuance and custody, marking a structural increase in institutional involvement.

Globally, stablecoin regulations are being rapidly implemented, with steady progress observed in Hong Kong, the United Kingdom, South Korea, Singapore, and the United Arab Emirates.

Trump said during the Oval Office signing event that companies cannot be expected to seek approval from dozens […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-limits-states-ability-to-regulate-ai/

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $77.3419 million yesterday, with Fidelity FBTC experiencing the largest net outflow at $104 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $77.3419 million yesterday (December 11th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $76.7054 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $62.68 billion. This was followed by Bitwise ETF BITB, with a net inflow of $8.4408 million, and BITB's historical total net inflow has reached $2.289 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of $104 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $12.177 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $119.925 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.55%, and a cumulative net inflow of $57.855 billion.

data.title: Yi Lihua: Remains firmly bullish on Ethereum
data.description: Yi Lihua, in an article published on X, cited a recent report from Trend Research, expressing a continued bullish outlook on Ethereum. First, Wall Street consensus is strengthening: the SEC Chairman's latest statement on "finance migrating on-chain" indicates that US political and economic elites are jointly promoting the tokenization of US Treasury bonds, with Ethereum as the core platform. Second, the Fusaka upgrade is reshaping value: Blob fees have surged, burning over 1500 ETH in a single day, accounting for 98% of the total. L2 prosperity is strongly benefiting the mainnet, and deflation is imminent. Third, the technical picture is extremely clean: speculative leverage has fallen to a historical low of 4%, and exchange holdings are only 10%. ETH/BTC is consolidating and showing resilience, short sellers are exhausted, and a short squeeze is imminent. In the interest rate cut cycle, funds are rotating from BTC to ETH, which has practical value. The original report can be viewed at "Trend Research: The 'Blockchain Revolution' is Underway, Continued Bullish Outlook for Ethereum".

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $42.3734 million yesterday, with only the 21Shares ETF TETH experiencing a net inflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (December 11th, Eastern Time), Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $42.3734 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the 21Shares ETF TETH, with a net inflow of $2.0845 million. TETH's historical total net inflow has reached $23.2565 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a net outflow of $31.2175 million. ETHE's historical total net outflow has reached $5.005 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $20.309 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.22%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $13.108 billion.

Apple was held in contempt by Judge Gonzalez Rogers who tagged its actions a “willful violation” and a “cover-up” […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/apple-loss-in-epic-app-store-appeal/

data.title: Bitcoin Accumulators Increase Holdings Amid Market Conditions
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, CryptoQuant analyst DarkFrost has reported that Bitcoin 'accumulators' have increased their holdings by over 75,000 Bitcoins from the 1st to the 10th of this month. Notably, between the 9th and 10th alone, they accumulated 40,000 Bitcoins. These addresses currently hold approximately 315,000 Bitcoins, and this upward trend continues. These investors appear to be unaffected by current market conditions or momentum.

DarkFrost further outlined the characteristics of these 'accumulator' addresses: they show no outflows, have a minimum purchase amount in their most recent transaction, have engaged in at least two purchase events (inflows), must hold a minimum total Bitcoin balance, must have been active at least once in the past seven years, exclude known exchange and miner addresses, and do not involve smart contracts.

data.title: Ark Invest increased its holdings of Bitcoin spot ETF ARKB by 13,700 shares and Robinhood by over 120,000 shares.
data.description: According to Ark Invest Daily monitoring, Ark Invest's funds increased their holdings of the Bitcoin spot exchange-traded fund ARKB by 13,700 shares. Specifically, the ARKW fund bought 4,496 shares, and the ARKF fund bought 9,204 shares. In addition, Ark Invest also significantly increased its holdings of Robinhood stock, with the ARKK fund buying 96,048 shares and the ARKW fund buying 28,379 shares, totaling over 120,000 shares.

data.title: The U.S. Congress is urging the SEC to allow Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies to be included in 401(k) retirement plans.
data.description: The U.S. Congress is urging the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to approve the inclusion of Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies in 401(k) retirement plans. Members of the House Financial Services Committee sent a letter to SEC Chairman Paul Atkins urging the SEC to update securities rules to treat digital assets as an equivalent investment class to other alternative investments in retirement accounts. The letter argues that Americans saving for retirement deserve more investment options, and that current rules are outdated and overly restrictive, preventing millions from accessing new asset classes. Furthermore, the letter emphasizes the need to redefine the criteria for "accredited investors." Current stringent investor eligibility requirements limit participation in parts of the private and alternative investment markets. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: Solana has officially confirmed details of XRP's login to this network, with support from Hex Trust and LayerZero.
data.description: Solana officially announced on the X platform that Hex Trust and LayerZero_Core will bridge and issue wXRP, a token supporting Solana DeFi, which can be exchanged on the XRP Ledger at a 1:1 ratio at any time. Previously, it was reported that Hex Trust planned to issue and host a wrapped version of XRP, "wXRP," to expand multi-chain DeFi applications.

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer from Bitgo Custody to DeFi Dev Corp
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Arkham data reveals that at 12:05, a transfer of 60,900 SOL occurred from Bitgo Custody to DeFi Dev Corp. This transaction highlights the movement of substantial cryptocurrency assets between these entities.
